response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a long and varied software development career spanning web, mobile, and some embedded work, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. He lacks native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), has no demonstrated experience in network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), no Linux kernel development or modification experience, and his primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js rather than C for UNIX/embedded systems. His brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in his thesis project is insufficient for mid-senior embedded firmware ownership.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across multiple domains and languages
Some C/C++ background from National Instruments and academic work, showing familiarity with lower-level programming
Linux exposure across multiple roles, indicating baseline OS familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement for the role
No experience with any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, device attack detection, encryption key exchange)
No embedded networking, L2/L3 switching, kernel development, or chip-vendor SDK experience; career is almost entirely web and application-layer work
